title: video_player
objectives: Manage buffering, disposal and playback state

sections.markdown: `video_player` (Flutter Team) plays videos via ExoPlayer (Android) and AVPlayer (iOS). It's the primitive; for a full-featured player with controls, wrap it with `chewie` or `better_player`.

For HLS/DASH streams (adaptive bitrate), plain `video_player` works but has limits — `better_player` exposes more knobs (cache, subtitles, ABR). For scale, use a real video CDN and preheat next-video decoders.
Skipping `dispose()` — a huge leak because decoders are heavy. Also: creating a new controller in `build()` — one controller per video, cached.
Wrap with Chewie for defaults you'd otherwise reimplement (play/pause, scrubbing, fullscreen). Use `visibility_detector` to pause auto-play videos scrolled out of view.

flashcards.back: ExoPlayer on Android, AVPlayer on iOS.

flashcards.front: Where do you get UI controls?
flashcards.back: Wrap the controller in Chewie (or better_player).
Missing dispose() or building the controller inside build().

quiz.q: For controls out of the box…

challenges.text: Play a network URL with default controls (Chewie).
Auto-pause when scrolled off-screen via visibility_detector.
Prefetch + cache next N clips in a TikTok-style feed.

interviewQuestions: How do you prevent decoder leaks?
